tactical_shape: Retained the same basic shape as Lamouchi
Tightened things up after the break, but only after Japan had already taken control
standout_players: Aymen Dahmen replaced Mouhib Chamakh in goal; made a sprawling save to deny Takehiro Tomiyasu before half-time
Dylan Bronn made a last-gasp clearing challenge to stop Japan increasing their lead early
Montassar Talbi was beaten for Ayase Ueda's second goal
Mohamed Amine Ben Hmida was caught playing Ito onside for Japan's third
home_read.momentum: A heavy defeat leaves Tunisia out already; the punditry frames them as a side in disarray, with little evidence the coaching change has altered the underlying problems.

tactical_shape: Poured forward in waves with tactical tweaks from the draw with the Netherlands
Used the left side well early, with Keito Nakamura crossing low from a wingback position
Conserved energy and played within themselves in the second half once in control
standout_players: Ayase Ueda inspired Japan, scored twice and led the line with intelligence and imagination
Daichi Kamada scored the opener, albeit via a goal off his heel after a low cross
Keito Nakamura created the opener from the left
Takefusa Kubo was absent through injury
away_read.momentum: A very good side with a landmark win and record margin for an Asian team at the World Cup; Japan look confidently set up and in control of their group fate.
